solves.essence: Reverse integer by iteratively appending digits while checking for overflow
solves.time_complexity: O(log|x|) — because we process each digit of the input number once
solves.title: Reverse Integer

solves.essence: Use two pointers to track unique elements in a sorted array
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the number of elements in the array, because we are scanning the array once
solves.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array

solves.essence: Remove elements from an array in-place using two pointers
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the number of elements in the array, because we are potentially scanning the entire array once
solves.title: Remove Element

solves.essence: Utilize Python's built-in string method to find the index of the first occurrence of a substring
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the haystack string, because in the worst case the find method has to scan the entire string
solves.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

solves.approach: Floyd's Tortoise and Hare (Cycle Detection) algorithm
solves.essence: Determine if a number is happy by detecting cycles using Floyd's algorithm
solves.time_complexity: O(log n) — because the number of digits in n is logarithmic in n, and each iteration reduces the number of digits
solves.title: Happy Number

solves.essence: Reverse a string in-place using two pointers
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string, because we are scanning the string once
solves.title: Reverse String

solves.approach: Two Pointers with Sorting and Set
solves.essence: Find unique intersection of two arrays using sorted sets and two pointers
solves.time_complexity: O(n log n + m log m) — due to sorting the sets of size n and m
solves.title: Intersection of Two Arrays

solves.essence: Return intersection of two arrays with duplicate counts preserved
solves.time_complexity: O(n log n + m log m) — due to sorting both input arrays of lengths n and m
solves.title: Intersection of Two Arrays II

solves.essence: Use two pointers to traverse both strings and check for subsequence
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of string t, because we are potentially scanning through the entire string t once
solves.title: Is Subsequence

solves.essence: Reverse the first k characters for every 2k characters in a string
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string, because we are scanning the string once
solves.title: Reverse String II

solves.essence: Reverse each word in a string while preserving whitespace and initial word order
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the input string, because we are scanning the string once
solves.title: Reverse Words in a String III

solves.essence: Separate even and odd numbers using two pointers
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the number of elements in the array, because we are doing a single pass through the array
solves.title: Sort Array By Parity

solves.essence: Count binary substrings with consecutive 0's and 1's by tracking previous and current character counts
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string, because we make a single pass through the string
solves.title: Count Binary Substrings

solves.essence: Check if a string can be a palindrome after deleting at most one character
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string, because we potentially traverse the string twice
solves.title: Valid Palindrome II
Shortest Distance to a Character
solves.essence: Calculate shortest distance to a character in a string by making two passes from left to right and right to left
solves.time_complexity: O(n) — where n is the length of the string, because we make two passes through the string
